globalConsistencyRules: All images use the same clean editorial 2D illustration style as the style reference.
The same named person must keep the same face, hair, skin tone, build, and wardrobe family.
The same named room or workplace must keep the same architecture, fixtures, furniture, and recurring props.
Scene images may change camera angle, crop, and foreground composition, but not identity or location continuity.
actionAssetRules: Every action asset must be generated on a single pure white #FFFFFF background.
Every action asset must have thin black or very dark charcoal outlines around the object silhouette and key internal edges.
No transparent checkerboard, colored backdrop, cast shadow, gradient, texture, label, checkmark, or completion badge should appear unless the real object requires readable text.

jordan_ellis.name: Jordan Ellis
jordan_ellis.sourcePersona: Jordan is a practical municipal planner who can clarify process, code expectations, and common staff concerns. They are helpful but careful not to promise approval or negotiate final conditions during a pre-application call.
jordan_ellis.appearancePrompt: Jordan Ellis, Current Planner, City of Eastbank. Use this as the locked character identity: Jordan is a practical municipal planner who can clarify process, code expectations, and common staff concerns. They are helpful but careful not to promise approval or negotiate final conditions during a pre-application call.. Choose one specific, plausible face shape, hairstyle, hair color, skin tone, age presentation, body type, wardrobe, and accessories, then keep them identical across all scenes.
jordan_ellis.wardrobePrompt: Keep the same job-appropriate wardrobe family unless the scene explicitly requires a change.

consulting-office.name: Consulting Office
consulting-office.appearancePrompt:
Create a stable location reference for Consulting Office. Keep the architecture, fixtures, furniture, wall layout, screens, doors, lighting, and recurring props consistent across every scene that uses this location. Source details: Welcome to VerdantWorks
First-person view of a modern planning and engineering consulting office. A laptop on a warm cream desk shows a project dashboard titled Riverbend Yard Feasibility Sprint with tabs for Client Brief, Zoning, Parcel Map, Comment Log, Meeting Prep, and Client Email. Printed parcel maps, zoning sticky notes, and a 3:30 PM pre-application call reminder sit nearby. The tone is professional, practical, and deadline-driven.
